chunks.page_summary: This document provides assembly guidelines and a detailed description of the Rheonics APC Adjustable Process Connector, an accessory designed for flexible sensor probe connection and installation.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

APC Adjustable Process Connector - Assembly Guideline

Modified on: Thu, 30 May, 2024 at 9:31 PM

Rheonics APC - Adjustable Process Connector

The Rheonics APC is a flexible process connection system that can be used to clamp an industrial flange, connector, or adapter to the Rheonics SRV and SRD sensor probe's body without welding. The APC allows a flexible insertion length (if PTFE back ferrule is used), easy exchange of process connections, and easy assembly and disassembly of the sensor probe from the process.

This accessory is used for flanges and any other process connections that meet the APC use requirement. It can also be used as an accessory to clamp the probe to external structure.

Review more on the Rheonics APC web page (https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/apc/).

Notes for assembly

*   The APC is designed for ambient pressure applications.
*   The flange or adapter is either supplied by Rheonics or the final user.
*   The back ferrule BF-APC should be ordered of PEEK (-PT) material when the process connection needs to be moved. Metal back ferrule (-SS) can be ordered when location of the process connection is desired to be fixed.
*   O-rings used in the assembly (OR-APC and FS-APC) are of FKM. Consider compatibility of fluid with this material.
key_facts: The Rheonics APC is a flexible process connection system.
It can clamp an industrial flange, connector, or adapter to Rheonics SRV and SRD sensor probes without welding.
The APC enables a flexible insertion length when a PTFE back ferrule is utilized.
It facilitates easy exchange of process connections and simple assembly and disassembly of the sensor probe from the process.
The accessory is suitable for flanges and other process connections meeting APC requirements.
It can also function as an accessory to clamp the probe to an external structure.
The APC is designed for ambient pressure applications.
The flange or adapter can be supplied by Rheonics or the end-user.
A PEEK (-PT) back ferrule (BF-APC) should be ordered for movable process connections.
A metal (-SS) back ferrule can be ordered for fixed process connection locations.
O-rings (OR-APC and FS-APC) used in the assembly are made of FKM material.
warnings_notes: Consider compatibility of fluid with FKM material used for O-rings (OR-APC and FS-APC).

chunks.page_summary: This page provides an exploded view and a detailed parts list for the Rheonics APC accessory, outlining its components, their descriptions, and a critical warning regarding its use in pressurized applications.
chunks.extracted_text:
The APC accessory should NOT be used for sealing and/or connection of probe inline for pressurized applications.

Rheonics APC - Exploded View

Figure 1: Rheonics APC on Viscometer SRV - Exploded parts view

key_facts: The provided table details all parts used in the assembly and use of the Adaptable Process Connection.
PTFE back ferrules (BF-APC-PT) are used for variable insertion of the probe.
Stainless Steel back ferrules (BF-APC-SS) are used to fix the position of the APC on the probe.
diagram_labels: Inline Process Viscometers and Density meters
warnings_notes: The APC accessory should NOT be used for sealing and/or connection of probe inline for pressurized applications.

key_facts: The TN-APC is a Wedge Lock Nut.
The TN-APC has a Female BSPP G 1 1/4 thread.
The TN-APC has a hex size of SW 55 mm.
The OR-APC is an O-Ring with dimensions Ø42x2.5 mm.
The MB-APC is an APC Mandrel Body.
The FS-APC is a Front Seal O-Ring with dimensions Ø30x2.5 mm.
Process Connection or Flange with a machined hole is an optional component for APC assembly.
Interface drawings of flanges or adapters for use with APC are available on a webpage.
warnings_notes: Optional: Process Connection or Flange with machined hole.
Review webpage for interface drawing of flanges or adapters to be used with APC.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions with accompanying illustrations for assembling components of a Rheonics sensor, including seals, mandrels, O-rings, and process connections.
chunks.extracted_text:
N°	Instruction	Picture
1	Place the APC seal (FS-APC) in the inner groove of the APC mandrel (MB-APC).
2	Insert APC mandrel (MB-APC) on the sensor probe body and place the O-ring (OR-APC) right behind the conical section of the mandrel.
3	Insert the Process Connection (flange) or any adapter required.

Text found within diagrams:
MB-APC
FS-APC
OR-APC
Flange - Process Connection with machined hole Φ 42.2
See interface drawing
rheonics (logo)
key_facts: The APC seal (FS-APC) must be placed in the inner groove of the APC mandrel (MB-APC).
The APC mandrel (MB-APC) needs to be inserted onto the sensor probe body.
The O-ring (OR-APC) should be placed right behind the conical section of the APC mandrel.
The Process Connection (flange) or any required adapter is to be inserted.
The Process Connection flange has a machined hole with a diameter of 42.2 mm.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step assembly and disassembly instructions for components of an APC system, detailing the use of threaded hex nuts, back ferrules, and compression nuts with corresponding visual aids.
chunks.extracted_text:
N° Instruction Picture
4 Thread hand tight the threaded hex nut (TN-APC) to clamp the flange against the mandrel (MB-APC) and O-ring (OR-APC).
SW 55mm
5 Place the conical back ferrule (BF-APC-PT or -SS) behind the APC mandrel (MB-APC).
6 Thread hand tight the compression nut (CN-APC) to fix the APC mandrel (MB-APC) on the sensor probe body.
To disassemble and change the position of the APC (for BF-APC-PT), unthread the compression nut (CN-APC).
SW 50mm
Resources
APC Accessory Page https://rheonics.com/product-accessories/apc/
key_facts: The threaded hex nut (TN-APC) is used to clamp the flange against the mandrel (MB-APC) and O-ring (OR-APC).
The conical back ferrule (BF-APC-PT or -SS) should be placed behind the APC mandrel (MB-APC).
The compression nut (CN-APC) fixes the APC mandrel (MB-APC) on the sensor probe body.
To disassemble or change the position of the APC (for BF-APC-PT), the compression nut (CN-APC) must be unthreaded.
warnings_notes: Components should be threaded hand tight during assembly to prevent over-tightening or damage.
